figures.caption: Fig. 1: AlphaJet architecture. The genetic algorithm outer loop (red blocks) maintains a population of anatomical vectors. Each candidate is regularized by the AD-VAE prior, projected onto the user envelope, and voxelized analytically (blue blocks). The voxel grid is scored by a transparent multi-physics evaluator (green blocks), and the best individual of every generation is streamed to a browser viewer.

Fig. 2: Live AlphaJet browser viewer showing the best individual of the current generation alongside its per-metric fitness breakdown. The voxel cloud, anatomical parameter vector, and physics diagnostics are streamed over WebSocket and updated every generation.
